action_metadata.metadata_title: Withdraw 540,750 ada for Oura by TxPipe: Maintaining Cardano’s Event Pipeline

onchain_context:
onchain recipient history: stake address stake1784sdxt6jjennmstphgdu7l7c2scf5d02a6cve2dgn5s2kq5u3j9v appears in 15 withdrawal proposals totalling 177273260000000 lovelace (first 2026-06-23 16:14:33, last 2026-07-09 16:30:25)
onchain DRep vote counts so far: yes=60 no=14 abstain=4 (explicit ballots, not stake-weighted)
treasury flow (36 epochs): inflow 158689112947739 lovelace, enacted withdrawals 291435336000000 lovelace, ratio 1.836518

fact_verdicts.note: Source Material 0 states action id b3d452bff7769d7f557ec6b8974760ee6c5e496c276652b654032966621e0ccf#7, type TreasuryWithdrawals, the exact title, 540750000000 lovelace, proposed epoch 638, expires after 645. All values match.

Anchor body.onChain.gov_action.rewards: key stake1784sdxt6jjennmstphgdu7l7c2scf5d02a6cve2dgn5s2kq5u3j9v, value "540750000000"; policy_hash fa24fb305126805cf2164c161d852a0e7330cf988f1fe558cf7d4a64. Exact match.
Anchor motivation: "requests 420,000 ADA (plus a 105,000 ADA contingency reserve) to fund **a part-time maintainer for Oura** ... over 12 months at an annual rate of $105,000 USD."
Anchor Budget Summary table: WP1 - Oura Maintenance and Enhancement 525,000; Intersect Budget Administration fee 15,750; Total 540,750. Exact match.
Anchor motivation: "Oura is a Rust-native pipeline tool that connects to Cardano nodes, monitors blockchain events, and routes matching patterns to pluggable outputs for processing." Verbatim.
Anchor motivation lists sources (N2C, N2N, S3, Mithril, UTxO RPC) and sinks (Kafka, Elasticsearch, AWS Lambda/S3/SQS, RabbitMQ, Redis, SQL databases, webhooks, and others). Exact match.
Anchor motivation: "With 654 commits, 288 stars, 79 forks, and 58 contributors..." and "The project remains fully open-source." Numbers match.
Anchor motivation: "Oura has been funded twice through Project Catalyst (Fund 9 and Fund 13) for feature development."
Anchor Prior Treasury Funding Disclosure: 30 Catalyst proposals across Funds 9 through 14, 26 delivered, 4 under development and on schedule. Exact match.
Anchor Prior Treasury Funding Disclosure names Intersect's 2025 budget cycle funding for Pallas, Dolos, and UTxO RPC, "all of which are being delivered on schedule."
Anchor Intersect Budget Process section: "achieved the required 67% support threshold during the 2026 Intersect Budget Process Hydra Voting phase."
Anchor Net Change Limit Compliance section states exactly this, citing the 350M NCL covering Epoch 613 to Epoch 713 and guardrail TREASURY-02a.

Anchor Audit & Oversight section: overhead includes audit and oversight costs; independent oversight via Intersect and technically capable third-party, with reporting obligations and milestone-based disbursement controls.
Anchor: Intersect will utilize the Sundae Labs treasury management smart contract framework, "a single Treasury Reserve Smart Contract (TRSC), with one Project-Specific Smart Contracts (PSSC)."
Anchor: "The 2026 Treasury Reserve Smart Contract stake address: stake1784sdxt6jjennmstphgdu7l7c2scf5d02a6cve2dgn5s2kq5u3j9v" — matches the reward account in gov_action.
Anchor: "The Oversight Committee consists of Sundae Labs, Cardano Foundation, Dquadrant, NMKR, Sundial and Eternl." All six names match.

Anchor Processes section: "funding for this project will be transferred using the Fund action to the PSSC. All milestones will be outlined within the metadata." The supplied anchor text indeed contains no milestone schedule — the absence is verifiable within the material.
Source Material 1 states 15 withdrawal proposals totalling 177273260000000 lovelace, first 2026-06-23 16:14:33, last 2026-07-09 16:30:25, for that stake address. Exact match.
Source Material 1: "onchain DRep vote counts so far: yes=60 no=14 abstain=4 (explicit ballots, not stake-weighted)." Exact match.
Source Material 1: treasury flow (36 epochs) inflow 158689112947739, enacted withdrawals 291435336000000, ratio 1.836518. Exact match.

values_hierarchy.md orders: 1 constitutional integrity/protocol safety, 2 treasury stewardship and downside protection, 3 evidence quality and reproducibility, 4 public-benefit ecosystem growth, 5 execution speed and social consensus momentum. The stated relative ordering is correct.
treasury_spending_doctrine.md, Dossier Gate Posture: "Hard gate (current): incomplete independently verified diligence means NEEDS_MORE_INFO, never an automatic NO."

treasury_spending_doctrine.md, Counterfactual risk bullet: for established infrastructure the engine evaluates ecosystem cost of non-funding, workflow dependency, replacement difficulty, sustained operation, and verified adoption alongside spending risk.

values_hierarchy.md conflict-handling default: "If treasury upside is plausible but non-verifiable: judge directionally on the available public record with a caution penalty (dossier soft gate...) — in practice NO or ABSTAIN on thin asks."

verification.summary: All 24 dossier facts are supported by the supplied material, with no contradictions and no invented figures, names, dates, or commitments. Facts 1-2 and 18-20 reproduce the on-chain action metadata and context block exactly (action id, 540750000000 lovelace, epochs 638/645, policy hash, recipient history, vote counts, treasury-flow figures). Facts 3-17 track the anchor document, including every checkable number (420,000 + 105,000 ADA, $105,000/yr, 525,000 / 15,750 / 540,750 ADA, 654/288/79/58 GitHub stats, 30 Catalyst proposals with 26 delivered and 4 in progress, the 67% Hydra threshold, and the 350M NCL over Epochs 613-713) and every named entity (the six Oversight Committee members, the Sundae Labs framework, the TRSC stake address). Facts 21-24 restate the doctrine accurately. One non-material note for the reader, not a discrepancy against any fact: the doctrine material itself carries two gate postures — treasury_spending_doctrine.md describes a hard gate (NEEDS_MORE_INFO) while values_hierarchy.md describes a soft-gate caution-penalty path (NO or ABSTAIN on thin asks) — and facts 22 and 24 each quote their respective source correctly. Which posture governs the vote is a judgment call for the decision-maker, not an accuracy defect in the dossier.
